name: 8bit-nes
title: 8-BIT NES
description: Cross-framework NES-arcade design system: CSS design tokens + class recipes + light-DOM web components. Zero build, dark-only, square 90° corners, hard shadow.

rules: Square 90° corners everywhere — never add border-radius or a chamfer.
Depth = hard shadow (box-shadow: Npx Npx 0), never blur.
One accent per block: set data-accent on the container; children inherit via --accent.
Dark text on bright accent fills (var(--ink-on-accent)); never light text on a solid fill.
Every value comes from a token in tokens.css — never hard-code hex/px in a component.
Prefer native elements/controls; add ARIA only to fill gaps.

concepts.name: Introduction
concepts.description: A cross-framework component system with the feel of a 1985 arcade cabinet — square pixel boxes, hard shadows, chiptune, and XP. Modern-crisp, dark-only, zero build.

concepts.name: Installation
concepts.description: Add the package, import the CSS once, register the web components. Three environments, one result.

concepts.name: Theming
concepts.description: One file is the source of truth. Change the look in tokens.css — never in a component.

concepts.name: Colors
concepts.description: The full palette. Grounds, ink, and a base + deep pair for every brand and semantic accent — all tokens in tokens.css.

components.name: Button
components.description: Primary action. Green (the primary accent) by default; set data-accent to recolor. Presses in on :active — the signature move.

components.name: Badge
components.description: Static status marker. Not interactive — for state you can read at a glance.

components.name: Chip
components.description: Toggleable filter or tag. Interactive — presses in, fills with its accent when active.

components.name: Card
components.description: Surface for grouped content. A left accent bar classifies it; hard border, hard shadow, square corners.

components.name: Avatar
components.description: User image or initials. Square by design so the hard shadow stays crisp; group them to overlap.

components.name: Kbd
components.description: Keyboard key hint. Inline, mono, inset — for shortcuts in prose.

components.name: Separator
components.description: Divider between content. Solid or dashed; horizontal or vertical.

components.name: Icon
components.description: Pixel icon set on a 16×16 grid. currentColor + 1em, so icons inherit text color/accent and scale with font-size. Vector — razor-sharp at any DPR (low-res & retina). Tree-shakeable named exports.

components.name: Input
components.description: Single-line text field. Recessed on the screen surface; focus flips the border to gold.

components.name: Textarea
components.description: Multi-line text. Vertically resizable, same recessed treatment as Input.

components.name: Select
components.description: Native select, restyled. Keeps the OS keyboard and mobile picker behaviour; gold chevron.

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: Boolean toggle in a list. Square box with a pixel check when on.

components.name: Radio
components.description: One-of-many choice. Same square box, filled solid when selected.

components.name: Switch
components.description: On/off setting with immediate effect. Green (good) accent by default.

components.name: Field
components.description: Label + control + hint/error wrapper. The .err modifier turns the whole field critical.

components.name: ColorPicker
components.description: Native color input, restyled as a square chip. The OS picker stays; only the swatch is themed.

components.name: InputDate
components.description: Native date input in the 8-bit shell. Keeps the OS calendar; the picker glyph is tinted to the primary.

components.name: InputTime
components.description: Native time input, same shell as InputDate. OS clock picker, tinted glyph.

components.name: CheckboxGroup
components.description: A <fieldset> that groups related checkboxes under one legend. Add .row to lay them out horizontally.

components.name: RadioGroup
components.description: Same .control-group fieldset, holding radios that share a name (single choice).

components.name: InputNumber
components.description: A − [n] + stepper over a native number input. <nes-number> wires the buttons; value clamps to min/max/step.

components.name: InputRating
components.description: Star picker. Click or arrow-key to set; add readonly for a display-only score. size=lg enlarges it.

components.name: PinInput
components.description: One-time-code / PIN entry: N single-char cells that auto-advance, accept a full paste, and fire nes:complete.

components.name: InputTags
components.description: Type + Enter (or comma) to add a chip; × or Backspace removes. Value is the ordered tag list.

components.name: FileUpload
components.description: Click-or-drop zone over a native file input. Lists chosen files with a remove button; supports multiple + accept.

components.name: Listbox
components.description: An always-open selectable list with roving focus. Single by default; add multiple for many. Options via child JSON.

components.name: InputMenu
components.description: Free-text combobox: type to filter suggestions, or keep any typed value. Autocomplete without locking the input.

components.name: SelectMenu
components.description: Strict searchable single-select: a trigger shows the label; the popup adds a filter box; the value must be one of the options.

components.name: Form
components.description: A thin wrapper that runs native constraint validation, renders inline errors under each .field, and emits nes:submit with the data.

components.name: Alert
components.description: Inline message with a semantic tone. Four shades map to meaning, not decoration.

components.name: Progress
components.description: Determinate bar. Fills with a smooth ease so progress glides — striped so the amount reads at a glance.

components.name: Skeleton
components.description: Loading placeholder. The shimmer ticks in steps rather than gliding.

components.name: Toast
components.description: Transient confirmation. Fires from JS, auto-dismisses, and is announced politely.

components.name: Tabs
components.description: Switch between panels. Roving focus and arrow keys, built from [data-label] children.

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.description: Path to the current page. Mono, chevron-separated, last item current.

components.name: Pagination
components.description: Page through a list. The current page fills with the primary accent; ends disable.

components.name: Modal
components.description: Focus-trapping dialog on the native <dialog> element. Opens with showModal().

components.name: Dropdown
components.description: Disclosure menu on the native <details> element. Zero JavaScript.

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Label on hover or focus. Pure CSS via a data-tip attribute — no JavaScript.

components.name: Table
components.description: Rows of data. Mono uppercase headers, hover highlight, scrolls on overflow.

components.name: Code block
components.description: Drop code in — <nes-code> highlights it and wires the copy button. No manual markup, no dependency.

components.name: Accordion
components.description: Collapsible section. <nes-collapsible> — the header button toggles the body.

components.name: Stat
components.description: A single big number with a label. Tabular figures; accent-colored.

components.name: Range
components.description: Slider on a native <input type=range>. Recessed track, square accent thumb. Set data-accent to recolor.

components.name: Segmented control
components.description: A row of connected buttons for a single choice — the pressed one fills with the accent. Great for a difficulty or view switch.

components.name: Spinner
components.description: A square loader whose lit edges spin smoothly — a loader with no curve. Three sizes; accent-ready.

components.name: Meter
components.description: A discrete segmented gauge — HP / energy in blocks. Add .on to filled cells; good/warn/crit read as health states.

components.name: Empty state
components.description: The "no data" / "game over" panel. Square dashed edge, centered mono copy, and always an action out.

components.name: Steps
components.description: Stage progression. Mark cleared stages .done and the active one aria-current — reads as NES "STAGE 1 · 2 · 3".

components.name: Drawer
components.description: A side sheet on the native <dialog>. Slides in from the edge with showModal(); add .start to come from the left.

components.name: Rating
components.description: A read-only score as a row of stars, filled up to .on. Gold by default; recolor with data-accent.

components.name: Number stepper
components.description: A − [n] + control around a native number input. Wire the buttons to step the value.

components.name: Banner
components.description: A full-width, page-level notice — distinct from the inline .callout box. Accent-tinted, optional dismiss.

components.name: Nav list
components.description: Vertical sidebar navigation for app shells. Mark the current item with aria-current; recolor with data-accent.

components.name: Description list
components.description: Key → value rows for a detail panel or settings summary. Built on native <dl>.

components.name: Timeline
components.description: Vertical events on a connector line with square markers. Great for activity feeds and changelogs.

components.name: Prose
components.description: Drop rendered markdown / CMS HTML inside — it restores semantic rhythm, square bullets, and accent links.

components.name: Tree
components.description: Hierarchical folder/file list. Expand/collapse, single or multiple selection, full keyboard nav. Data via child JSON.

components.name: Chat
components.description: The chatbot shell: a flex column with a scrolling message area and a prompt pinned to the bottom. Compose it from the parts below.

components.name: ChatMessages
components.description: Scroll container that auto-sticks to the newest message while streaming — but won't yank the view if the reader scrolled up.

components.name: ChatMessage
components.description: One message row: an avatar + a square bubble. .assistant sits left on the panel; .user mirrors right and tints with the accent.

components.name: ChatPrompt
components.description: The input: an auto-growing textarea + send button. Enter sends, Shift+Enter = newline. Fires nes:submit with the text, then clears.

components.name: ChatPromptSubmit
components.description: The send/stop button used inside ChatPrompt. Add .busy to turn the green send into a red stop. Usable standalone too.

components.name: ChatReasoning
components.description: A collapsed "thinking" block for reasoning tokens. Native <details> — zero JS. Dashed, mono, muted; the summary goes gold when open.

components.name: ChatTool
components.description: Shows a tool/function call: name + status badge in the summary, args/result in the body. Native <details>; status via a .badge.

components.name: ChatShimmer
components.description: Streaming placeholder — a few shimmering lines shown while the assistant's first tokens are still on the way. Pure CSS.

components.name: ChatPalette
components.description: A framed chat panel for a modal or side-panel assistant: bordered surface with the messages area above and the prompt flush on the bottom edge.

components.name: Editor
components.description: Lightweight rich-text editor on native contenteditable (zero deps). Toolbar, slash/@/: menus, block drag, VSCode-style Tab autocomplete, and an AI hook — for second-brain / knowledge / blog apps.

components.name: EditorToolbar
components.description: The format bar built into <nes-editor>: bold/italic/underline/strike, H1/H2, lists, quote, inline code, link, and an AI (✦) button. Scrolls horizontally on mobile.

components.name: EditorSuggestionMenu
components.description: The slash (/) command menu — the Notion-style way to insert blocks. Type / to open, keep typing to filter, ↑/↓ + Enter to pick.

components.name: EditorMentionMenu
components.description: The @-mention menu for referencing notes / people / entities — the backbone of a second-brain link graph. Options come from the editor's JSON config.

components.name: EditorEmojiMenu
components.description: The colon (:) emoji picker — type :name to filter a built-in set and insert the emoji. No dependency, no image sprite.

components.name: EditorDragHandle
components.description: A grip (⠿) that appears at the start of the hovered block; drag it to reorder blocks. Pure HTML5 drag — no library.

components.name: Typography
components.description: Render an AI agent's streamed Markdown / MDC output as beautiful, on-brand HTML — prose embeds (::code-preview, ::code-group, ::field-group…) that map onto real components.

components.name: CodePreview
components.description: Live rendered result on top, its source below — the ::code-preview embed. The “here's the component, here's the code” block.

components.name: CodeGroup
components.description: Tab between several code blocks in one frame — the ::code-group embed. Perfect for pnpm/npm/yarn or multi-language snippets.

components.name: CodeCollapse
components.description: Hide a long code block behind a toggle — the ::code-collapse embed. Native <details>, zero JS.

components.name: CodeTree
components.description: File tree on the left, syntax-highlighted viewer on the right — the ::code-tree embed. Ideal for rendering a repo or multi-file snippet an agent generated.

components.name: CardGroup
components.description: A responsive grid of cards that reflows by itself — the ::card-group embed. Drop any number of .card children in.

components.name: FieldGroup
components.description: A bordered list of prop / parameter rows — the ::field-group embed. The clean way to render an API or config reference.

components.name: Prompt
components.description: A single terminal / AI command line with an accent caret (❯) — the ::prompt embed. For showing commands or an agent's input.

components.name: Visualize
components.description: Turn an AI agent's diagram output (Mermaid) into on-brand visuals, and teach a concept step-by-step. Mermaid is never bundled — bring your own or lazy-load it.

components.name: Mermaid
components.description: Render a Mermaid diagram themed to the system. Streaming-safe (set .code per chunk) and AI-safe (strict). Mermaid isn't bundled.

components.name: Walkthrough
components.description: Step-by-step 'How it works'. Prev/Next + arrow keys; each step spotlights part of a diagram so a principle unfolds one piece at a time.

components.name: Lens
components.description: See one concept from many angles — flow, sequence, data, trade-offs — as tabs. Pure composition over Tabs; deepens understanding.
